# Work Orders

**Total Story Points:** 89 | **Sprints:** 6 | **Stories:** 18

---

## Epic 1: Foundation & Infrastructure
> Establish the Next.js project scaffold, database schema, authentication, and image pipeline so all feature development has a stable, production-ready foundation.

### [E1-S1] Project Scaffold & Environment Setup
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 1 | **Points:** 3
**Tags:** backend, frontend, infrastructure, devops

**User Story**
As a developer, I want a fully configured Next.js 14 project with all core dependencies installed so that the team can begin feature development without environment blockers.

**Work Description**
Initialize a Next.js 14 App Router project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Prisma ORM, and NextAuth.js. Configure ESLint, Prettier, and path aliases. Set up Vercel project with preview and production environments, and wire `.env` files for all third-party service keys (Supabase, S3, Stripe, Resend, Imgix).

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] `next dev` runs without errors and renders a placeholder homepage at `localhost:3000`
- [ ] Prisma is connected to a Supabase PostgreSQL instance and `prisma migrate dev` executes successfully with an initial empty migration
- [ ] Vercel project is linked to the repository; pushes to `main` auto-deploy to production URL and PRs generate preview URLs
- [ ] All environment variables are documented in `.env.example` with placeholder values and a setup README section
- [ ] Tailwind CSS and Framer Motion are verified working via a temporary test component that is removed before merge

---

### [E1-S2] Database Schema & Prisma Models
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 1 | **Points:** 5
**Tags:** backend, database

**User Story**
As a developer, I want a complete, type-safe database schema so that all application data — artwork, orders, commissions, blog posts — has a consistent and relational structure from day one.

**Work Description**
Design and implement the full Prisma schema covering all domain entities: `Artwork`, `Category`, `Product` (shop item), `Order`, `OrderItem`, `CommissionRequest`, `BlogPost`, `Subscriber`, and `User` (admin). Define all relations, required fields, and indexes. Run the initial migration against Supabase and verify Prisma Client generates without errors.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] `schema.prisma` defines all 9 models with correct field types, relations, and `@unique`/`@index` decorators on query-critical fields (e.g., `slug`, `email`)
- [ ] `prisma migrate dev --name init` applies cleanly to Supabase with zero errors
- [ ] Prisma Studio (`npx prisma studio`) opens and displays all tables with correct columns
- [ ] Seed script (`prisma/seed.ts`) inserts at least 3 sample artworks and 1 admin user, runnable via `npm run db:seed`
- [ ] All models include `createdAt` and `updatedAt` timestamps with `@default(now())` and `@updatedAt` respectively

---

### [E1-S3] AWS S3 & Imgix Image Pipeline
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 1 | **Points:** 5
**Tags:** backend, infrastructure

**User Story**
As the artist, I want uploaded artwork images to be stored safely and served at the correct resolution for any device so that pages load fast without sacrificing visual quality.

**Work Description**
Configure an AWS S3 bucket with appropriate CORS and bucket policy for private uploads. Build a Next.js API route (`POST /api/upload`) that generates a pre-signed S3 URL for direct browser-to-S3 uploads. Set up an Imgix source pointed at the S3 bucket. Create a reusable `<ArtworkImage>` React component that constructs Imgix transformation URLs (width, format=webp, quality) based on viewport context using `next/image` with a custom Imgix loader.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] `POST /api/upload` returns a pre-signed S3 URL valid for 5 minutes; uploading a file via that URL results in the object appearing in the S3 bucket
- [ ] Imgix source is configured and a test image URL (`{imgix-domain}/test.jpg?w=800&fm=webp&q=75`) returns a resized WebP image
- [ ] `<ArtworkImage>` component renders images using the Imgix loader and passes `sizes` prop correctly for responsive breakpoints
- [ ] A Lighthouse performance test on a page using `<ArtworkImage>` shows no render-blocking image issues and images are served as WebP
- [ ] S3 bucket blocks all public access; images are only accessible through the Imgix CDN domain

---

### [E1-S4] NextAuth.js Admin Authentication
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 1 | **Points:** 3
**Tags:** backend, frontend, security

**User Story**
As the artist, I want a secure login to access my admin dashboard so that no one else can modify my portfolio, shop, or commission data.

**Work Description**
Implement NextAuth.js with Credentials provider (email + bcrypt-hashed password) backed by the Prisma `User` model. Create a `/admin/login` page with a React Hook Form + Zod-validated login form. Protect all `/admin/*` routes using Next.js middleware that checks the session token and redirects unauthenticated users to `/admin/login`. Store sessions in Upstash Redis.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] `POST /api/auth/signin` with valid credentials returns a session cookie; invalid credentials return a 401 with an error message
- [ ] Navigating to any `/admin/*` route without a valid session redirects to `/admin/login`
- [ ] Login form displays field-level validation errors for empty or malformed email/password before submission
- [ ] Session persists across browser refreshes and expires after 7 days of inactivity
- [ ] Password is stored as a bcrypt hash (min 10 rounds); plaintext password is never logged or returned by any API route

---

## Epic 2: Portfolio Gallery
> Build the public-facing gallery and artist-managed upload system so artwork is displayed beautifully, organized by category, and fully manageable without developer involvement.

### [E2-S1] Public Gallery Page with Filtering
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 2 | **Points:** 8
**Tags:** frontend, backend

**User Story**
As an art buyer or collaborator, I want to browse the artist's work filtered by medium or series so that I can quickly find the style I'm looking for without scrolling through everything.

**Work Description**
Build the `/gallery` page using a CSS masonry grid layout with Tailwind CSS. Fetch artworks server-side via a Prisma query scoped by `isPublished: true`. Implement client-side filtering by `Category` (medium, style, series) using URL search params so filtered views are shareable and indexable. Each artwork card shows a thumbnail via `<ArtworkImage>`, title, and medium. Clicking a card opens a full-screen lightbox with zoom capability using a library such as `yet-another-react-lightbox`.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Gallery page renders all published artworks in a responsive masonry grid (1 column mobile, 2 tablet, 3 desktop)
- [ ] Clicking a category filter updates the URL query param (e.g., `?category=watercolor`) and filters the displayed works without a full page reload
- [ ] Each artwork opens in a lightbox with pinch-to-zoom on mobile and scroll-to-zoom on desktop
- [ ] Gallery page achieves a Lighthouse performance score ≥ 85 on mobile with 20+ artwork thumbnails loaded
- [ ] Artworks with `isPublished: false` are never returned by the gallery API route, verified by a direct API test

---

### [E2-S2] Individual Artwork Detail Page
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 2 | **Points:** 5
**Tags:** frontend, backend

**User Story**
As an art buyer, I want to view full details of a specific artwork — including high-resolution image, dimensions, medium, and purchase availability — so that I can make an informed buying decision.

**Work Description**
Create a dynamic route at `/gallery/[slug]` using Next.js `generateStaticParams` for SSG with on-demand revalidation. The page displays the full-resolution Imgix image, artwork title, year, medium, dimensions, description, and availability status. If the artwork has an associated `Product` record in the shop, display price and an "Add to Cart" CTA. Include structured data (`application/ld+json` for `VisualArtwork`) for SEO.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Page is statically generated at build time for all published artworks and revalidates within 60 seconds of an admin update
- [ ] Page URL uses the artwork's unique `slug` field (e.g., `/gallery/autumn-reverie-2024`)
- [ ] `VisualArtwork` JSON-LD schema is present in the page `<head>` and validates cleanly in Google's Rich Results Test
- [ ] If the artwork is for sale, an "Add to Cart" button is visible and functional; if sold or unavailable, a "Not Available" badge is displayed instead
- [ ] Navigating directly to a non-existent slug returns a Next.js 404 page

---

### [E2-S3] Admin: Artwork Upload & Management
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 2 | **Points:** 8
**Tags:** frontend, backend, admin

**User Story**
As the artist, I want to upload new artworks with metadata through a simple form so that I can keep my portfolio current without needing a developer.

**Work Description**
Build an `/admin/artworks` management page listing all artworks in a sortable table with publish/unpublish toggles. Create an artwork create/edit form (`/admin/artworks/new` and `/admin/artworks/[id]/edit`) using React Hook Form + Zod with fields for: title, slug (auto-generated from title), category (multi-select), medium, dimensions, year, description, availability, and image upload. The image field triggers the pre-signed S3 upload flow from E1-S3 with a progress indicator. On submit, call `POST /api/admin/artworks` or `PATCH /api/admin/artworks/[id]`.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Artist can upload a JPEG or PNG up to 50MB; a progress bar shows upload status and the form prevents submission until the upload completes
- [ ] Slug field auto-populates from the title input but remains manually editable; duplicate slugs are rejected with an inline error
- [ ] Publish/unpublish toggle on the list page calls `PATCH /api/admin/artworks/[id]` and updates `isPublished` without a full page reload
- [ ] Deleting an artwork soft-deletes the record (`deletedAt` timestamp) and removes it from public gallery; the S3 object is not deleted (for safety)
- [ ] All form fields are validated client-side (Zod schema) and server-side (API route re-validates); invalid payloads return 400 with structured error messages

---

## Epic 3: E-Commerce & Commission System
> Deliver a complete purchase flow for original works and prints, plus a structured commission inquiry system, so the artist can generate revenue directly through the site.

### [E3-S1] Product Catalog & Shopping Cart
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 3 | **Points:** 8
**Tags:** frontend, backend

**User Story**
As an art buyer, I want to add artworks or prints to a cart and review my order before paying so that I can purchase confidently from a single artist directly.

**Work Description**
Build the `/shop` page displaying `Product` records (originals, prints, digital downloads) with price, availability, and category. Implement a client-side cart using Zustand with localStorage persistence. Each product has a detail page at `/shop/[slug]`. The cart drawer shows line items, quantities (for prints/downloads), subtotal, and a checkout CTA. Cart state syncs between tabs via `storage` event listeners.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Products are listed with title, thumbnail, price, and stock status; out-of-stock items show a disabled "Sold Out" button
- [ ] "Add to Cart" button adds an item to the Zustand store and opens the cart drawer with a count badge on the cart icon
- [ ] Cart persists across page refreshes using localStorage; items are restored correctly on revisit
- [ ] Removing an item from the cart updates the subtotal instantly without any API call
- [ ] Cart correctly enforces quantity limits: original artworks cap at quantity 1; print/download quantities are editable up to available stock

---

### [E3-S2] Stripe Checkout & Order Processing
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 3 | **Points:** 8
**Tags:** backend, payments, security

**User Story**
As an art buyer, I want to pay securely for my cart items using my credit card or PayPal so that I can complete a purchase without worrying about the safety of my payment details.

**Work Description**
Implement `POST /api/checkout/create-session` which creates a Stripe Checkout Session from the cart line items, applying product prices fetched server-side (never trusting client-sent prices). On Stripe redirect back, handle `checkout.session.completed` webhook at `POST /api/webhooks/stripe` to create an `Order` + `OrderItem` records, decrement product stock, and trigger a Resend order confirmation email. Build a `/checkout/success` and `/checkout/cancel` page. Add PayPal as a secondary option using `@paypal/react-paypal-js`.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Clicking "Checkout" calls `/api/checkout/create-session`; the response redirects the browser to the Stripe-hosted checkout page within 2 seconds
- [ ] Product prices used in the Stripe session are fetched from the database server-side; submitting a manipulated cart payload with altered prices does not affect the charged amount
- [ ] Stripe webhook at `/api/webhooks/stripe` validates the `stripe-signature` header; tampered or missing signatures return 400 and do not create orders
- [ ] A successful payment creates an `Order` record with status `PAID`, decrements `Product.stock` atomically, and dispatches a Resend confirmation email to the buyer within 30 seconds
- [ ] `/checkout/success` page displays order summary including order ID, items purchased, and total charged, fetched via the Stripe session ID in the URL

---

### [E3-S3] Admin: Order Management
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 3 | **Points:** 5
**Tags:** frontend, backend, admin

**User Story**
As the artist, I want to view all incoming orders with buyer details and fulfillment status so that I can ship physical works and deliver digital files without losing track of anything.

**Work Description**
Build `/admin/orders` listing all orders with columns for order ID, buyer email, items, total, status (`PAID`, `FULFILLED`, `REFUNDED`), and date. Clicking an order opens a detail view showing full line items, buyer shipping address, and a status update dropdown. Implement `PATCH /api/admin/orders/[id]` to update status. When status changes to `FULFILLED`, trigger a Resend fulfillment notification email to the buyer using a React Email template.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Orders list is paginated (20 per page) and sortable by date descending by default
- [ ] Artist can filter orders by status using tab buttons (All / Paid / Fulfilled / Refunded); filter updates the list without a full page reload
- [ ] Updating an order to `FULFILLED` status triggers a Resend email to the buyer's address within 60 seconds, verified by checking Resend's dashboard logs
- [ ] Order detail page displays all `OrderItem` records including artwork title, variant (original/print), quantity, and unit price
- [ ] Attempting to access `/admin/orders` without a valid admin session returns a 302 redirect to `/admin/login`

---

### [E3-S4] Commission Request System
**Type:** feature | **Priority:** high | **Sprint:** 4 | **Points:** 8
**Tags:** frontend, backend

**User Story**
As a potential client, I want to submit a detailed commission request with my concept, size, budget, and timeline so that the artist has everything needed to respond with an accurate quote.

**Work Description**
Build a `/commissions` public page explaining the commission process (how it works, turnaround, pricing guidance). Include a structured React Hook Form + Zod-validated request form with fields: name, email, artwork concept (textarea), preferred medium (select from artist-defined options), size (width × height inputs), budget range (select

core_features.name: Portfolio Gallery
core_features.description: A visually rich, categorized gallery to display artwork with filtering by medium, style, or series. Supports high-resolution image uploads with zoom capability.

core_features.name: Online Shop / Art Store
core_features.description: E-commerce functionality allowing the artist to sell original pieces, prints, or digital downloads with secure payment processing via Stripe or PayPal.

core_features.name: Commission Request System
core_features.description: A structured inquiry form where clients can describe custom artwork requests, specify size and medium, set a budget, and receive automated status updates.

core_features.name: About & Bio Page
core_features.description: A personalized page showcasing the artist's story, influences, resume/CV, press mentions, and exhibition history to build credibility and personal brand.

core_features.name: Contact & Social Integration
core_features.description: A contact form with email notifications and integrated links to social platforms (Instagram, Pinterest, Behance) to drive cross-channel engagement.

core_features.name: Blog / Process Journal
core_features.description: A content section where the artist can share behind-the-scenes posts, work-in-progress updates, and art tips to build audience connection and improve SEO.

core_features.name: Admin Dashboard
core_features.description: A simple CMS-style backend allowing the artist to upload new works, manage shop inventory, view commission requests, and track orders without technical knowledge.

core_features.name: Analytics & Visitor Insights
core_features.description: Integrated analytics (Google Analytics or equivalent) to track page views, popular artworks, traffic sources, and conversion rates on shop items.

core_features.name: Newsletter / Mailing List Signup
core_features.description: Email capture functionality integrated with Mailchimp or similar to allow fans and buyers to subscribe to new releases, exhibitions, and announcements.

core_features.name: Mobile-Responsive Design
core_features.description: Fully optimized mobile and tablet experience ensuring artwork displays beautifully and all functionality — including purchasing — works seamlessly on all devices.

technical_constraints: High-resolution image optimization required to balance quality and page load speed
Secure payment gateway integration (PCI compliance for e-commerce)
SEO-friendly architecture for discoverability of artist name and artwork
CMS must be non-technical and easily managed by the artist independently
SSL certificate required for secure transactions and user trust
Storage and bandwidth must support a growing library of high-res artwork files
success_metrics: Artist can upload and manage portfolio without developer assistance
At least 1 completed online sale within the first 30 days of launch
Portfolio page load time under 3 seconds on mobile devices
Commission inquiry form receives at least 5 submissions per month
50+ unique monthly visitors within 60 days of launch
Email newsletter list reaches 100 subscribers within 90 days
Artist reports 80%+ satisfaction with ease of site management
